Patch Set 38:

> > Patch could be simplified if we dropped the special handling for
 > having a single preferred nid. Is it worth keeping that code?
 > 
 > s/Patch/This code.../

Agree/disagree?

Patch Set 38:

Chris, I agree that the code would get simplified if special handling for the case of a single preferred nid is dropped. I'm not sure if it is worth dropping. I can only guess, but the originator must have been concerned with the overhead of dealing with the lists when it is not necessary. My personal preference would be implement a more uniform solution. What do you think?

parents.subject: LU-9121 lnet: UDSP liblnetconfig structure def

commit.subject: LU-9121 lnet: foundation patch for selection mod
commit.message:
LU-9121 lnet: foundation patch for selection mod

Add the priority and preferred NIDs fields in the lnet_ni,
lnet_net, lnet_peer_net and lnet_peer_ni. Switched
the implementation of the preferred NIDs list to list_head
instead of array, because the code is more straight forward.
There is more memory overhead due to list_head, but these lists
are expected to be small, so I chose code simplicity over memory.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=lnet-selftest,sanity-lnet
